_meta.description: Full mechanical specification for every Mutants & Masterminds 4e skill, for implementing skill-check resolution and character logic. All 'summary' and 'use.effect' fields are original paraphrases of the rulebook's functional rules (DCs, opposed traits, degree-of-success outcomes, timing) — not copied prose. Companion to skills.json (short always-visible tooltip copy + core metadata) and mm-skills-design.md (this file's pattern definitions and cost/limit rules). Read mm-skills-design.md first for the resolution-pattern ids referenced in each use's 'pattern' field.

abilityKey.ATK: Attack (combat ability, not a core ability)

categoryKey.combat: Bonus applies to attack checks; capped by the Attack PL limit, not the skill-bonus PL limit
categoryKey.interaction: Social skill; requires a shared understood language and a subject with mental abilities; -5 if the subject can't hear/understand; usable on groups only for a single shared result
categoryKey.manipulation: Requires fine physical manipulation (an AGL and STR rank plus use of limbs, or a Precise effect); penalty if hands are Impaired/Disabled
categoryKey.technical: Requires tools; -5 without them (except routine 'operate' uses); covers operate/construct/repair/disable

resolutionPatternKey.static_dc: Check vs a fixed or table-driven DC set by task difficulty
resolutionPatternKey.opposed_check: Check vs a named opposing trait's check result (or its routine 10+bonus)
resolutionPatternKey.resistance_check: Skill substitutes for a resistance check to avoid/overcome an effect
resolutionPatternKey.impress_check: An Impress action (see mm-actions-adventure.md) that imposes a condition on a target on success; target re-checks at end of each of its turns
resolutionPatternKey.attack_maneuver: Feeds a combat maneuver (Feint/Trick/Grab/Escape/Block etc.) resolved in the actions layer
resolutionPatternKey.opposed_hide: Stealth vs Perception to become/stay unnoticed; gated by Cover/Concealment
resolutionPatternKey.extended_construct: Multi-step build/repair/disable with build-time ranks and per-degree time reduction
resolutionPatternKey.attack_bonus: Flat bonus to attack checks with a configured focus (Close/Ranged Combat)
resolutionPatternKey.knowledge_check: Expertise/Magic-style question answering; routine-eligible; GM often rolls in secret
resolutionPatternKey.gm_secret: GM makes the check secretly; the player doesn't learn the raw result
resolutionPatternKey.narrative: Resolution is a GM narrative call, not an engine-computed number

_meta.costRule: 1 Power Point per 2 skill ranks. Ranks bought as a narrow Specialization cost half as much: 1 PP per 4 specialized ranks, but each specialization is its own independent rank pool. Expertise is inherently specialization-priced (4 ranks/PP) because a focus is mandatory.
_meta.skillBonusLimit: Total skill bonus (ability rank + skill rank + advantage/power bonuses, EXCLUDING circumstance modifiers) is capped at PowerLevel + 10. Two documented exceptions: Combat skills (Close/Ranged Combat) are governed by the Attack PL limit instead, and Languages is uncapped except at GM discretion. See power-progression.json's maxSkillModifier column.
_meta.degreeOfSuccessNote: Many uses branch on degrees of success/failure. Convention used in 'use.effect' text: '1 success' = met DC; '2+ successes' = beat DC by 5+; '1 failure' = missed by up to 4; '2+ failures' = missed by 5+. The engine reads degrees from dice.py's resolution, never recomputes them per-skill.
_meta.untrainedNote: trainedOnly=true skills auto-fail untrained. Some skills are partly trainedOnly: individual uses carry 'trainedOnly' to override the skill-level flag (e.g. Investigation's Searching is usable untrained even though the skill is Trained Only overall).

skills.name: Acrobatics
skills.summary: Balance, tumbling, contortion, and acrobatic movement under difficult conditions.

uses.name: Balancing
uses.effect: Check to keep balance during hazardous movement. 1 failure: you spend your Move action just staying balanced and don't advance. 2+ failures: you lose balance and fall. You are both Hindered and Vulnerable while balancing; you can ignore either condition for +5 to the DC, or both for +10. If you fail a resistance check while balancing, make an immediate Balancing check at the original DC or fall.

dcTable.example: surface half a Space wide or wider

modifiers.when: chosen to not be Hindered while balancing
chosen to not be Vulnerable while balancing

uses.name: Contorting
uses.effect: DC 25 as part of a Move action to squeeze through a gap wide enough for your head but not your shoulders, or reach through an opening too narrow for your arm. 1 failure: no progress this turn. 2+ failures: can't retry without Extra Effort and may become stuck/Immobilized (GM's call) until a DC 15+ Acrobatics check frees you.

uses.name: Maneuvering
uses.effect: DC 15 to ignore the Hindered condition from certain terrain by acrobatically moving through it. 1 failure: Move action spent with no progress. 2+ failures: fall Prone.

uses.name: Standing
uses.effect: DC 20 to take the Stand action (removing Prone) without becoming Hindered that round. 1 failure: you Stand but are Hindered. 2+ failures: simple action wasted, remain Prone. The Instant Up advantage does this automatically with no check.

uses.name: Tumbling
uses.effect: DC 5 to lessen fall damage, reducing it by 1 rank per degree of success. A fall reduced to rank 0 does no damage and you land on your feet as a free action; otherwise you end Prone. Safe Fall (Enhanced Movement) or Immunity to Falling Damage ignore falls entirely.

skills.name: Athletics
skills.summary: Physical feats — climbing, jumping, running, swimming.

uses.name: Climbing
uses.effect: Check to climb a slope/wall/incline. 1 failure: no progress. 2+ failures: you fall from your attained height unless secured. If you fall, an Athletics resistance check at (initial DC + 10) catches you; someone in reach can also attempt it at the same DC. You are Vulnerable while climbing unless you accept +5 to the DC. A perfectly smooth vertical surface needs the Wall-Crawling effect. No tools where tools are needed: -5.

dcTable.example: an uneven surface such as a rock face
a rough surface such as a brick wall

modifiers.when: can brace against two opposite walls (duct/chimney)
corner with perpendicular walls to brace against
climb of your size rank + 1 in distance or less
climbing +1 speed rank faster (up to full ground speed)
chosen to not be Vulnerable while climbing

uses.name: Jumping
uses.effect: Base jumps without a check: running long jump = distance rank 0 (move Speed 1 then jump as one Move action); standing long jump = distance rank -1; vertical jump = distance rank -2. Each degree of success on a DC 15 check adds +1 to the jump's distance rank, to a maximum of +3. The Leaping effect greatly improves this.

uses.name: Running
uses.effect: DC 15 as part of a Move action to run faster: success gives +1 ground speed rank until the start of your next turn. 1 failure: no change. 2+ failures: collide and fall Prone (or simply fall Prone if the path is clear). The Speed effect greatly improves ground speed.

uses.name: Swimming
uses.effect: DC 10 to swim at speed rank 0 (higher DC for rough water). Each degree of success past the first adds +1 swim speed rank for one round, up to your ground speed rank (normally 2). 2+ failures: you go under and must hold your breath to avoid drowning. The Swimming effect improves swim speed; Immunity to Drowning removes the risk.

modifiers.when: rescuing another character who cannot swim

skills.name: Close Combat
skills.summary: Attack-check bonus with one configured type of close attack; each focus is its own ranked skill.
specializations: by specific weapon or attack, e.g. Hammers, Knives, Swords

uses.name: Close Attack Bonus
uses.effect: Adds your skill rank to attack checks with the configured focus only — not to defenses, and not to attacks outside that focus. Each focus is a separate Close Combat skill with its own rank. Unlike ordinary skills, this bonus is bounded by the Attack Power Level limit (attack + effect rank ≤ PL×2), not the skill-bonus limit. 'Unarmed' covers punches/kicks only; grabbing and tripping have their own focuses. For a broader attack bonus see the Close Attack advantage; for a per-power one see the Accurate extra.

skills.name: Deception
skills.summary: Convincing lies, distraction, covert messages, and combat trickery.

uses.name: Bluffing
uses.effect: Tell a believable lie; opposed by the target's Deception or Insight. The resistance modifier scales with how hard the lie is to believe and how much risk it demands of the target. If the target beats you by 10 or less on a risk-boosted deception, they merely balk at the risk rather than seeing through it; by 11+ they've seen through it outright.

modifiers.when: believable and doesn't affect the target much
a little hard to believe or puts the target at some risk
hard to believe or entails serious risk

uses.name: Distracting
uses.effect: Impress check to distract an opponent: on success the target is Vulnerable to your attacks, Defenseless with 2+ successes. Target re-checks at the end of each of its turns to shed the condition. Can also be used as an opposed check (vs the higher of target's Deception or Insight) to distract someone enough to let you Hide.

uses.name: Innuendo
uses.effect: Send covert messages hidden in ordinary conversation. The intended recipient must make a Deception or Insight check at the same DC to understand it. Eavesdroppers may attempt Insight vs the message DC to notice it (2+ successes to also understand it). 2+ failures on the receiver's check means the message is misinterpreted.

dcTable.example: message with new or detailed information

uses.name: Resisting
uses.effect: You may substitute your Deception skill for resistance checks against Deception attempts made on you, since you know the tricks.

uses.name: Tricking
uses.effect: Use Deception in combat for a Trick maneuver, misleading an opponent into an unwise action. Resolved by the Trick action in the actions layer.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.summary: Alter your appearance with makeup, costume, and props; opposed by observers' Perception.

uses.name: Create Disguise
uses.effect: Your Disguise check result is the DC of observers' Perception checks (rolled by the GM secretly, on first encounter and periodically after). Impersonating a specific person gives observers who know them a bonus to notice: regular associates +2, friends +5, intimates +10. Acting convincingly as who you appear to be also needs a Deception check vs the observer's Deception or Insight (adjusted by familiarity). Takes ≥ time rank 6 (8 minutes) and can be routine; speed up 1 time rank per -5 to the Deception check. Morph greatly enhances this.

modifiers.when: insufficient props / disguising as someone very unlike you (up to -5)
observer is a regular associate of the impersonated person
observer is a friend of the impersonated person
observer is an intimate/loved one of the impersonated person

skills.name: Expertise
skills.summary: Deep knowledge/training in one field; each field is a separate skill; mandatory specialization pricing.
focuses: one field per instance, e.g. Law, Science, Journalism, Law Enforcement, Streetwise, Physician
skills.costNote: Because a focus is mandatory, Expertise is always priced at 4 ranks per Power Point (specialization rate), not 2.

uses.name: Knowledge
uses.effect: Answer a question within your field. DC 10 easy, 15 basic, 20+ difficult. Usually routine-eligible, and the GM may roll in secret. Covers everything except tasks specifically handled by other skills.

uses.name: Profession
uses.effect: Practice the field professionally: use its tools, do daily tasks, supervise untrained helpers, handle common problems. Most job checks are routine. Assumes any needed licensing/certification (licensing troubles are Complications).

uses.name: Technical Expertise
uses.effect: Some Expertise fields count as technical skills (needing tools; -5 without) allowing operate/construct/repair/disable of related items — e.g. Expertise: Chemistry or Cooking. A technical Expertise cannot replace a dedicated skill like Magic or Technology.

uses.name: Untrained Expertise
uses.effect: GM may allow untrained checks for broad 'general knowledge' areas, but only for easy/basic tasks (DC 10-15) and never as a routine check.

skills.notes: Ability modifier is usually INT but the GM may swap it per task (AGL for coordination-based job functions, AWE to notice details). A related Expertise may substitute for another skill at a -2 to -5 penalty at GM discretion. For 'knows everything' characters, pair high INT with the Know-It-All advantage.

skills.name: Insight
skills.summary: Read intentions and emotions; detect lies, influence, and social mood.

uses.name: Avoid Influence
uses.effect: Substitute an Insight resistance check to avoid or overcome certain interaction-skill effects; if you meet or beat your opponent's result you're unaffected, having seen through the attempt.

uses.name: Detect Falsehood
uses.effect: GM secretly checks whether you sense a statement or experience is false — essentially Avoid Influence for Deception, and also applies to sensing an Illusion/Hallucination or seeing through a disguise (opposed, or Insight vs the effect DC). Success tells you something is deceptive but not necessarily the truth.

uses.name: Detect Influence
uses.effect: Notice someone acting under outside influence. DC = 10 + the effect rank or skill affecting them. Success: you notice they aren't acting of their own free will. 3+ successes: a general idea of what (and maybe who) is influencing them.
uses.dcFormula: 10 + influencing effect rank or skill bonus

uses.name: Evaluate
uses.effect: Opposed by Deception, judge on meeting someone whether they're trustworthy. Or DC 20 to read a social situation's overall mood. 2+ failures: you misread the signs (so the GM may roll secretly).

uses.name: Innuendo
uses.effect: Use Insight to pick up hidden messages sent via Deception's Innuendo. Same DCs as that use.

skills.name: Intimidation
skills.summary: Threats to coerce cooperation or demoralize foes in combat.

uses.name: Coercing
uses.effect: Impress check to coerce cooperation: success treats the target's Attitude as Favorable (Helpful with 2+ successes) but only for actions taken in your presence. 2+ failures: the target may do the opposite. Win or lose, the target's true Attitude moves at least one step toward Hostile afterward.

uses.name: Demoralizing
uses.effect: Impress check in combat to undermine confidence: success makes the target Impaired, Frightened with 2+ degrees. Target re-checks at the end of each of its turns to remove the condition.

uses.name: Resisting
uses.effect: Substitute Intimidation for resistance checks against Intimidation attempts on you.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.summary: Search scenes, gather and analyze evidence, collect information, run surveillance.
skills.categoryNote: Gathering and analyzing evidence are technical (need tools); the other uses are not.

uses.name: Searching
uses.effect: Deliberately search an area for clues/hidden items/traps (Perception notices things instantly; Investigation finds them with effort). DC to find something deliberately hidden = the check result used to hide it. Search time = the time-measurement for the area's distance rank (e.g. an 8x8 ft room ≈ rank 1 = 1 minute; a square mile ≈ rank 10 = 2 hours).

dcTable.example: ransack an area to find a certain object
notice a secret compartment, simple trap, or obscure clue
find a well-hidden compartment/trap or extremely obscure clue (25+)

uses.name: Gathering Evidence
uses.effect: DC 15 to collect a piece of evidence for later analysis. 1 failure: analysis still possible but at -5. 2+ failures: evidence ruined, no analysis. 2+ successes: +5 bonus to the later analysis.

uses.name: Analyzing Evidence
uses.effect: DC 15 to extract information from evidence you already have (it does not create clues where none exist). Modified by elapsed time and scene disturbance. 2+ failures: misleading/confusing results (GM's call).

uses.name: Gathering Information
uses.effect: Spend ≥ an hour and make a check to pick up news and rumors. Degree of result sets how complete/protected the information is. 1 failure: waste time, learn nothing. 2 failures: you also alert someone interested in your questions.

dcTable.example: Restricted — not generally known; needs someone with access
Protected — hard and possibly dangerous to obtain

uses.name: Surveillance
uses.effect: Watch an area from a stationary spot: your Investigation result is the DC of a Stealth check to slip past your notice. For actively following a subject, use Stealth's Tailing instead.

skills.name: Languages
skills.summary: Fluency in languages beyond your native one; ranks double the count each step; not PL-capped.

uses.name: Fluency
uses.effect: Each rank grants fluency (spoken and literate) in a set of additional languages: 1 at rank 1, then doubling each rank (2, 4, 8, ...). Not limited by Power Level except at GM discretion. Rank 8 (128 languages) costs the same Power Points as the Comprehend Languages effect, which grants all languages, so there's little reason to go higher. Excess languages may be left unspecified and chosen when a new language is encountered (possibly via Edit Scene / a Hero Point).

skills.notes: Sharing an understood language is a prerequisite for interaction skills; lacking one imposes -5 on those checks.

skills.name: Magic
skills.summary: Arcane lore and the practical, technical craft of magic; not a substitute for magical powers.

uses.name: Lore
uses.effect: Answer questions about magical lore, creatures, or phenomena, using the same DC guidelines as Expertise's Knowledge. Unlike most Expertise, this cannot be used untrained (though a related Expertise may answer some questions at a penalty).

uses.name: Technique
uses.effect: Use Magic as a technical skill to study, repair, and disable magical items/devices (needs tools and time per the Technical Skills rules). The GM sets item complexity and whether you can create as well as repair/disable.

uses.name: Improvised Effects
uses.effect: With the Improvised Effects advantage on Magic, prepare and perform effects like rituals or limited-use items. Any character trained in Magic can also spend a Hero Point to gain the advantage for a single such effect.

skills.notes: Option: the GM may require a Magic focus (Alchemy, Necromancy, Sorcery, etc.) or offer those as specializations; a -2 penalty applies to tasks outside a character's focus.

skills.name: Perception
skills.summary: Notice things with your senses; opposed by Stealth or set against a visibility DC.

uses.name: Sight
uses.effect: Check vs a visibility DC or an opposed Stealth check. Plain sight is DC 0; subtle/overlooked things DC 5-10+. Also used to spot disguises and concealed objects.

uses.name: Hearing
uses.effect: Check vs a loudness DC or opposed Stealth. Normal conversation DC 0, soft noise DC 10, loud noises are negative DCs. Through a door +5, solid wall +15. Waking to a sound while asleep +10.

uses.name: Other Senses
uses.effect: Perceive with non-sight/hearing senses: obvious DC 0, less obvious DC 10, hidden DC 20+. Discerning fine detail needs 3+ degrees of success with an Acute sense. A general -1 applies per two Spaces of distance to the subject. Enhanced Senses provide extra options and modifiers.

modifiers.when: per two Spaces of distance to the subject

skills.notes: You make a Perception check automatically as a non-action whenever there's an opportunity to notice something new, unless Unaware. As a simple action you can deliberately observe something you missed. Acute perception (fine detail) needs 3+ degrees of success with an Acute sense.

skills.name: Performance
skills.summary: Impress or sway audiences through a configured performing art; each art is its own skill.

uses.name: Impressing
uses.effect: DC 15 is a competent performance; each further degree is more impressive. 4+ degrees wins over even a Hostile audience. A performance that impresses grants a bonus on later interaction checks with that audience: +2 for success, +5 for 3+ degrees.

uses.name: Swaying
uses.effect: Use Performance as an interaction skill to change Attitudes, using the same DCs and step-shift rules as Persuasion's Swaying.

skills.notes: Ability may be swapped from PRE at GM discretion (e.g. AGL for gymnastic Dance). Passive art forms (Drawing, Painting, Writing) are technical Expertise skills that create works, not Performance.

skills.name: Persuasion
skills.summary: Change Attitudes and win people over honestly; negotiation.
specializations: a particular social group or environment

uses.name: Swaying
uses.effect: Change a subject's Attitude. DC scales with how far below Helpful the subject currently is. Success shifts Attitude one step; every two further degrees shift another step. 1 failure: no change and you can't retry this scene. 2+ failures: undo prior success and worsen the subject's Attitude one step. Rushing: reduce the time rank by 1 per +5 accepted to the DC.

uses.name: Negotiating
uses.effect: All participants roll Persuasion to see who gets the best result; also resolves two advocates pleading opposing cases to a third party. Advanced negotiations use a check sequence.

skills.name: Ranged Combat
skills.summary: Attack-check bonus with one configured type of ranged attack; each focus is its own ranked skill.
specializations: by specific attack or weapon, e.g. Energy Blast, Pistols, Spears

uses.name: Ranged Attack Bonus
uses.effect: Adds your skill rank to attack checks with the configured focus only — not to defenses. Each focus is a separate Ranged Combat skill with its own rank. Bounded by the Attack Power Level limit, not the skill-bonus limit. For a broader ranged attack bonus see the Ranged Attack advantage.

skills.name: Sleight of Hand
skills.summary: Dexterous handwork — concealing, escaping restraints, close-up magic, planting, stealing.

uses.name: Concealing
uses.effect: Conceal a small item: your check result becomes the DC of an Investigation or Perception check to find it.

uses.name: Escaping
uses.effect: Slip out of restraints or Hindered/Immobilized conditions. Takes 1 minute (time rank 3) per check; each degree of success past the first reduces the time rank by 1. Also usable for an Escape action against a Grab. Restraint DCs: ropes 10, handcuffs 15, straightjacket 20; a power effect uses its effect DC.

uses.name: Legerdemain
uses.effect: Minor close-up magic (vanishing a coin/card): DC 10 unless observed, in which case your check is opposed by the observer's Perception.

uses.name: Planting
uses.effect: DC 20 to plant a small object on someone (slip into a pocket, drop a tracer). The target's Perception check notices your attempt if it beats yours, whether or not you succeed at planting.

uses.name: Stealing
uses.effect: DC 15 to covertly steal something from someone's possession. The target's Perception check notices your attempt if it beats yours, whether or not you succeed.

skills.name: Stealth
skills.summary: Go unnoticed; opposed by Perception; gated by Cover/Concealment.

uses.name: Hiding
uses.effect: With Cover or Concealment, Stealth vs an observer's Perception to hide. You can't hide from someone aware of you without a distraction or the Hide in Plain Sight advantage, and you can't hide with no Cover/Concealment at all. While using Stealth you are Hindered (-1 movement speed rank); remove the Hindered condition by taking -5 on the Stealth check.

uses.name: Hiding via Distraction
uses.effect: A successful Deception or Intimidation distraction lets you make a Stealth check while others are aware of you, at -5 (you must move quickly to Cover/Concealment).

modifiers.when: hiding after a distraction while observed

uses.name: Sneaking
uses.effect: Cover the sounds/signs of your movement, opposed by an observer's Perception (or their routine Perception if unalert).

uses.name: Tailing
uses.effect: Follow someone unnoticed at normal speed (needs some cover/concealment). Usually a check sequence of Stealth vs the subject's Perception.

skills.name: Survival
skills.summary: Endure and navigate the wilderness; forage; simple construction.
skills.categoryNote: Only the Simple Construction use is technical (needs tools; -5 without).

uses.name: Avoid Hazards
uses.effect: DC 15 to gain +2 to resistance checks against severe weather/natural hazards, or +5 if stationary under shelter. Extend the bonus to one extra person per point your result exceeds the DC.

uses.name: Foraging
uses.effect: DC 10 to move at ground speed rank -1 while finding food/water for yourself plus one extra person per 2 points over the DC.

uses.name: Navigation
uses.effect: DC 10 to navigate outdoors (15 if also avoiding hazards). Success keeps you on-course and roughly located.

uses.name: Simple Construction
uses.effect: Use Survival as a technical skill to build shelters/simple items, repair simple items, and set traps for defense or hunting. -5 without tools.

skills.name: Technology
skills.summary: Operate, construct, repair, and disable technology; improvised effects.

uses.name: Operating
uses.effect: Routine use of common equipment needs no check and can be untrained. Using an unfamiliar/advanced item needs a check: DC 10 simple to 25+ highly advanced.

uses.name: Constructing
uses.effect: Build an item from scratch over one or more DC 15 checks, one per degree of complexity. Each success advances (or completes) construction; each extra degree of success cuts the time rank by 1 (to a GM minimum). A failed check wastes that step's time and forces a restart of it; 2+ failures may cause an accident. -5 without tools.

complexityTable.examples: radio direction finder, lock, engine component

uses.name: Repairing
uses.effect: Repair a damaged item at base DC 10, +5 per degree of complexity above Simple, and -2 time rank vs building it. Each degree of success past the first cuts the repair time rank by 1. Failure wastes the time with no progress (keep trying); 2+ failures may worsen the damage. Jury-rigging: -5 to the DC and resolves as a standard action, but fixes only one problem and lasts until end of scene; must then be fully repaired and can't be jury-rigged again until it is.

uses.name: Disabling
uses.effect: Disable/shut down an item, usually ≥ 1 minute (time rank 3). DC by complexity: Simple 15, Moderate 20, Complex 25, Advanced 30, adjusted by security level. The GM rolls secretly. Success shuts it down without tripping security; extra degrees can cut time. 2+ failures may trip a security measure or cause a mishap.

uses.name: Improvised Effects
uses.effect: With the Improvised Effects advantage on Technology, prepare and perform effects like limited-use tech devices. Any character trained in Technology can also spend a Hero Point to gain the advantage for a single such effect.

skills.name: Treatment
skills.summary: Diagnose, provide care, revive, stabilize, and treat diseases/poisons.
skills.penaltyNote: -5 without medical equipment/supplies; possibly a further penalty for unusual biology; -5 when treating yourself (and self-treatment is limited to diagnose/care/disease-poison).

uses.name: Diagnosis
uses.effect: Takes ≥ 1 minute. DC 10 (higher for unusual conditions) to identify a subject's damage conditions. Success gives +2 to further Treatment checks for that condition, +5 with 3+ degrees.

uses.name: Revive
uses.effect: Remove Dazed (DC 10), Stunned (DC 15), or Incapacitated (DC 20) from a subject. You are Vulnerable until the start of your next turn while doing this. Success removes only that condition; other conditions and remaining damage persist. With the Improved Treatment advantage you can grant extra benefits on a successful revive.

uses.name: Stabilize
uses.effect: DC 15 as a standard action to stabilize a Dying character.

uses.name: Treat Ailments
uses.effect: Each time an afflicted character makes a resistance check against a disease/poison/ailment, you make a Treatment check: 1 success gives them +2 to that resistance check, 3+ successes gives +5.

skills.name: Vehicles
skills.summary: Operate ground, water, air, and space vehicles under pressure.

uses.name: Operate Vehicle
uses.effect: Routine operation of known vehicles needs no check and may be untrained for common vehicles (cars) but not uncommon ones (boats, aircraft). A check is needed only in stressful/dramatic situations — chases, attacks, racing a deadline. DCs and specific checks come from the Vehicles rules in the equipment layer.

skills.notes: Riding animal mounts is technically a separate Riding skill (AGL); most campaigns let Athletics stand in for it unless mounts are especially prominent.
